title: persona.update() dynamic SQL construction can silently update wrong columns
description: In persona.rs update(), the UPDATE SQL is built by appending column names from a Vec<&str> and then binding values in the same order. The code adds set clauses and then binds values in four separate if-let blocks. The order of the clauses and the order of the bindings must match exactly; if someone adds a new optional field and inserts the clause in a different position than the binding, the wrong value will be written to the wrong column with no compile-time or runtime error. This is a maintainability trap that will produce data corruption: e.g., bio value written to avatar_path.
close_reason: Fixed: persona.update() now uses single list pairing clauses with values.
